yesterdayStoryline.headline: Brazil held, Australia march, Scotland sneak past
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Brazil and Morocco share a goal apiece; Australia open with a 2-0 win; Scotland edge Haiti via Dykes.

todayStoryline.headline: Germany begin amid Curaçao's history-making debut
todayStoryline.subtitle: Group F opens with Netherlands–Japan; Germany face the smallest nation ever to qualify in Group G.

lines: Brazil struggled against Morocco's organized defense, resulting in a late equalizer.
The draw leaves Brazil needing a win in their next game to secure advancement.
Australia capitalized on Turkey's defensive lapses to secure a comfortable win.
This result boosts Australia's chances of advancing from Group D.

facts.home: Germany are still scarred from their 2018 World Cup group-stage exit — their first since 1938. Mesut Özil quit afterwards accusing the federation of racism over his Erdoğan photo.
facts.away: Curaçao is the smallest nation ever to qualify for a men's World Cup — population around 150,000, less than a London borough. Coached by Dutch veteran Dick Advocaat, one of football's most-travelled managers.

facts.home: The Netherlands' 2010 World Cup Final remains infamous — Nigel de Jong's chest-high kick on Xabi Alonso a low point. Referee Howard Webb showed a record 14 yellow cards as Spain won 1-0.
facts.away: Japan stunned Germany and Spain in 2022, but Kaoru Mitoma's assist for the Spain winner still sparks debate — the ball looked out, even FIFA's millimetre cam couldn't fully settle it.

facts.home: Ivory Coast won AFCON 2024 on home soil after firing manager Jean-Louis Gasset mid-tournament following a 4-0 loss to Equatorial Guinea. Interim Emerse Faé led the dramatic comeback to glory.
facts.away: Ecuador's 2022 qualification was nearly voided after Chile accused them of fielding Byron Castillo, claiming he was Colombian-born. FIFA fined them and docked future points but let them compete.

facts.home: Sweden missed the 2022 World Cup and Euro 2024, prompting Zlatan Ibrahimović's retirement at 41 after 121 caps and well-documented friction with coach Janne Andersson, who resigned shortly after.
facts.away: Tunisia stunned reigning champions France 1-0 in their 2022 World Cup group-stage finale — only the second time France had lost while holding the title. Tunisia still went home on goal difference.

news.headline: UN sanctions on arms sales to Myanmar
news.body: The UN Security Council imposed new sanctions targeting arms sales to Myanmar. The decision aims to curb escalating violence.

footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs
